title: A Fun Little Quiz Show
description: Adachi answers a quiz written by Shimamura—three questions about herself, her habits, and how Shimamura sees her. A playful, introspective look into Adachi's mind.
content:
A Fun Little Quiz Show

Adachi and Shimamura
— Bonus Content—

Question 1: If you were to compare Adachi to an animal, what would she be?

'Compare'… that word was what caught my eye first. Honestly, I'd rather not be compared to anything. I want her to answer with me—just me, as I am. I want her to accept the person standing in front of her without any metaphor. But since this is a question Shimamura came up with, I have to think it through properly too.

Me… as an animal… Even if I look in the mirror, I don't see any other creature reflected there. But sometimes—only sometimes, really just a little—I feel like I might resemble a dog.

On days when I get home first, I always trot over to the entryway the moment Shimamura comes in. When I do that, I even think I'm kind of dog-like. So, 'dog,' I wrote, my pen gliding across the answer column.

I couldn't think of anything else to compare myself to. Does anyone really have something like that? Shimamura, overall… she's more like a cat, I thought as I filled in the blank. The first question was pretty easy. Maybe, like my own quiz, things are just going to get harder from here.

Question 2: When Adachi falls asleep first, what kind of prank do I play on her?

This is the first I've heard of such a thing. A prank…? A prank? A prank by Shimamura. Just thinking about it made my forehead feel hot.

Lifting up my blanket while I'm asleep… The thought alone made me squat down and smack the table. My pen rolled back and forth across the paper with a soft clatter clatter. Shimamura still has this adorable childlike side to her, even as an adult. Maybe this is part of that. Watching her nearly untainted, crystal-clear heart always nourishes mine so much.

But—we're adults now. A prank, huh… When you say 'prank,' it feels like something you'd do at equal height, equal distance… What kind of prank…? Ah—right, this is the question itself… Just translating imagination into words was enough to let heat take over my head. This won't do. I'm way too far from being able to write an answer.

Wait—hold on. The condition is 'when Adachi falls asleep first.' Has something like that ever happened?

Shimamura falls asleep incredibly easily—something I envy. When we get into bed together, she's usually sleeping within minutes. Most of the time she goes to bed before I even do. So… that kind of opportunity shouldn't exist, right?

That realization made the heat in my head drain away like water rushing down my forehead. Meaning: this question is a trap. And more importantly—if Shimamura ever approached me, there's no way I wouldn't notice. Even when I'm asleep, I have absolute confidence in that. And if she touched me? I'd probably react like I'd been shocked by electricity. That's how desperately I'm always seeking Shimamura, waiting for Shimamura.

So the answer should be 'none.'

Probably. Maybe. Or maybe not. Maybe yes? I rummaged through the drawers of my memory up and down three whole times and found nothing at all. So I wrote 'none.'

Would it be better if she had played a prank…? If I kept wondering about that, I wouldn't be able to move on, so I shut my eyes, took a deep breath, and forcibly ended that line of thought.

Alright. Next question.

Question 3: In my heart, what do I actually call Adachi?

With my limited imagination, the only options that popped up were 'Adachi' and 'Sakura.' If there were any other possibilities, maybe she secretly calls me by some nickname…?

But I've never had a nickname, and no one's ever called me one, so I couldn't think of anything. If not a nickname, then that leaves 'Sakura'… But imagining Shimamura saying things like: 'Sakura, what are you up to?' 'Sakura, wake up.' 'Sakura, can you stand for a sec?' —felt strangely unfitting. It didn't match the atmosphere between us at all. So this one must be another trick question. Just like how, for me, Shimamura will always be 'Shimamura,' I believe she must think of me the same way. Certain of that, I wrote my own name—'Adachi'—in the answer column.
